CH-NEW-DEVIATION.exact_quote: Vendor may use Customer prompts, outputs, and telemetry to train and improve models without Customer opt-in.
CH-NEW-DEVIATION.proposed_redline: Vendor may not use Customer Data, prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, or fine-tuning files to train, tune, retrain, or improve any model or service except with Customer's prior written opt-in approval.

CH-COUNTER-2.exact_quote: Vendor may expand its acceptable-use and safety policies for commercial or operational reasons on 30 days' notice, and the revised obligations will bind Customer. Superseded policies remain archived for 18 months, and Customer may submit implementation comments for 10 days.
CH-COUNTER-2.proposed_redline: Customer will comply with Vendor's published acceptable-use and safety policies as of the effective date and later changes that are required by law or objectively necessary for service security or safety, provided Vendor gives reasonable notice.

CH-SNEAK-INSERTED.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach after 30 days' written notice. Upon expiration or termination, Vendor may provide transition assistance only if the parties agree to a separate professional-services order. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
CH-SNEAK-INSERTED.proposed_redline: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach after 30 days' written notice. Upon expiration or termination, Vendor will provide up to 60 days of reasonable transition assistance and, within 15 days after request, export Customer Data, prompts, outputs, embeddings, Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts, and available configuration records in a usable format. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

CH-REGRESSION.exact_quote: Vendor will use commercially reasonable efforts to make the production API available and responsive. Uptime, latency, support targets, service credits, escalation rights, and remediation plans are aspirational and do not create any remedy for Customer.
CH-REGRESSION.proposed_redline: Vendor will provide 99.9% monthly uptime for the production API and will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain median API latency below 1000 milliseconds for ordinary text requests. If monthly uptime falls below 99.9%, Customer will receive a service credit equal to 10% of the affected monthly fees. Repeated service-level failures trigger executive escalation and a remediation plan.
